exercises.title: Reading whole words
exercises.body: Now for the fun part — blending letters into words. Shavian is read strictly left to right, sound by sound, with no silent letters to trip you up. Look at 𐑛𐑪𐑑: 𐑛 (d) + 𐑪 (o) + 𐑑 (t) blends smoothly into "dot".

exercises.title: Why this alphabet exists
exercises.body: George Bernard Shaw thought English spelling wasted years of every child's life learning exceptions that taught nothing about how words actually sound. His will paid for a replacement — and blending 𐑛𐑪𐑑 straight into “dot”, no guessing required, is exactly the fix he had in mind.

exercises.title: One letter, not two
exercises.body: English often doubles up consonant letters — miss, add, well — but Shavian never does. One letter is always enough: 𐑨𐑛 says "add", not "addd".

exercises.caption: Sound it out — what does this say?
